name: CalFresh (SNAP)
description: California's food assistance program (SNAP/food stamps) providing monthly benefits on an EBT card to buy groceries for low-income individuals and families.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 200% of federal poverty level for gross income; net income at or below 100% FPL
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Vietnamese, Korean, and other languages

name: GetCalFresh
description: Simplified online application tool for CalFresh (SNAP) benefits, built by Code for America. Helps applicants complete the process in about 12 minutes from any device.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone eligible for CalFresh can use this simplified application portal
incomeLimitNotes: Same as CalFresh income limits
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Vietnamese, Korean, Tagalog

name: BenefitsCal
description: California's unified online portal to apply for and manage CalFresh, Medi-Cal, CalWORKs, and other county benefits programs for food, health coverage, and cash assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Any California resident can use this portal to apply for multiple public assistance programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Medi-Cal (Medicaid)
description: California's Medicaid program providing free or low-cost health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, dental, vision, mental health, and substance use disorder services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL (~$1,732/month for single person in 2026). Children up to 266% FPL.
languages: English, Spanish, and many other languages

name: Covered California
description: California's official health insurance marketplace where individuals and families can compare and enroll in health plans, often with financial help to lower monthly premiums.
eligibilitySummary: California residents not eligible for Medi-Cal or employer coverage who need health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Financial assistance available for households up to 400% FPL; enhanced subsidies may apply
hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Saturday 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM during open enrollment
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Korean, Vietnamese, Tagalog, and others

name: California EDD Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary partial wage replacement to workers who lose their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits range from $40 to $450 per week.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and earned enough wages during the base period
incomeLimitNotes: Benefits based on past earnings; $40-$450/week maximum

name: CalJOBS
description: California's online job search system where job seekers can create resumes, search for jobs, explore training programs, and connect with employers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all California job seekers

name: America's Job Center of California (AJCC)
description: Network of career centers managed by California's 45 Local Workforce Development Boards providing free job search help, training, resume assistance, and employer connections.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all job seekers and employers at no cost
incomeLimitNotes: Some training programs may have income eligibility requirements
hours: Varies by location; typically Monday-Friday business hours
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages vary by location

name: California Workforce Development Board (CWDB)
description: State board providing workforce assistance information and helping Californians find their local workforce development board or career center.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all California job seekers

name: California Housing Finance Agency (CalHFA)
description: State agency providing financing and home loan programs for low to moderate income Californians, including down payment assistance through the Dream For All program.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income first-time homebuyers in California
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by county and program; Dream For All targets first-generation homebuyers

name: California Department of Housing and Community Development (HCD)
description: State agency overseeing housing policy, rental assistance, homeless services, and affordable housing development programs across California.
address: 2020 W. El Camino Ave, Suite 500, Sacramento, CA 95833
eligibilitySummary: Varies by program; generally serves low-income Californians
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: HUD Rental Help in California
description: Federal HUD resource page for California listing local Public Housing Authorities, Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, and other rental assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families; income limits vary by county and household size
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 50-80% of area median income depending on program

name: LawHelpCA
description: Statewide portal for free legal help referrals maintained by the Legal Aid Association of California. Connects low-income Californians to nearly 100 nonprofit legal aid providers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income California residents needing civil legal assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125-200% of federal poverty level depending on the provider

name: State Bar of California - Free Legal Help
description: State Bar resource page listing certified lawyer referral services, legal aid organizations, and self-help resources for Californians who cannot afford an attorney.
eligibilitySummary: California residents seeking legal assistance, especially those with low or moderate incomes

name: Legal Aid Foundation of Los Angeles (LAFLA)
description: Nonprofit providing free civil legal services to low-income residents of Los Angeles County in areas including housing, family law, immigration, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Los Angeles County
incomeLimitNotes: Income less than 125% of federal poverty guidelines

name: Bay Area Legal Aid
description: Provides free civil legal help to low-income communities in the San Francisco Bay Area, including advice, self-help clinics, and full representation in housing, benefits, and family law matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of the San Francisco Bay Area (Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, Napa, San Francisco, San Mateo, and Santa Clara counties)
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 200% of federal poverty level or below
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, and other languages

name: California LIHEAP (Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Federally funded program helping low-income households pay heating and cooling costs. Provides bill payment assistance with benefits ranging from $94 to $1,500 depending on need.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 60% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: 60% of state median income; heating benefits $94-$1,500, cooling benefits $283-$932

name: CALIHEAPApply - Online Energy Assistance Application
description: California's official online portal for applying to the Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) for heating and cooling bill assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income California households needing help with energy bills
incomeLimitNotes: 60% of state median income

name: CARE/FERA Utility Discount Programs
description: California Alternate Rates for Energy (CARE) provides 30-35% electric bill and 20% gas bill discounts. Family Electric Rate Assistance (FERA) provides 18% electric bill discount for slightly higher-income families.
eligibilitySummary: CARE: households at or below 200% FPL or enrolled in qualifying public assistance programs. FERA: households of 3+ with income slightly above CARE limits.
incomeLimitNotes: CARE: up to 200% FPL. FERA: 201-250% FPL for households of 3 or more.

name: California Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Installs weatherization and energy efficiency measures (insulation, weather-stripping, furnace repair) in homes of low-income households to reduce energy costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Households earning 75% of state median income or less

name: 211 California
description: Statewide 2-1-1 information and referral service connecting Californians to health, human, and disaster services 24/7. Covers over 98% of the state's population.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all California residents
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpretation

name: 211 LA
description: Los Angeles County's 2-1-1 service providing information and referrals to health, human, and social services including shelter, food, housing, employment, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all Los Angeles County residents
languages: English, Spanish, and 140+ languages via interpretation

name: Los Angeles Regional Food Bank
description: Feeding America member food bank serving over 1 million people monthly across Los Angeles County through 600+ partner agencies. Has distributed over 2 billion pounds of food.
eligibilitySummary: Any person in Los Angeles County in need of food assistance

name: San Francisco-Marin Food Bank
description: Feeding America member food bank working to end hunger in San Francisco and Marin counties, distributing 67 million pounds of food annually with 60% being fresh produce.
eligibilitySummary: Any person in San Francisco or Marin County in need of food assistance

name: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley
description: Feeding America member food bank serving Santa Clara and San Mateo counties. Largest food bank in the Bay Area and 12th largest in the United States.
eligibilitySummary: Any person in Santa Clara or San Mateo County in need of food assistance

name: CalWORKs (California Work Opportunity and Responsibility to Kids)
description: California's TANF program providing temporary cash aid and supportive services to low-income families with children, helping them become self-sufficient through employment.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with children under 18 (or 19 if completing high school)
incomeLimitNotes: Income and asset limits vary by family size; very low-income families with few assets

name: CalWORKs Child Care (Stages 1, 2, and 3)
description: Three-stage child care subsidy program for CalWORKs families. Stage 1 during cash aid, Stage 2 transitioning off aid, Stage 3 for former recipients and other low-income families.
eligibilitySummary: Current or former CalWORKs cash aid recipients who are employed or in approved activities and need child care
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet CalWORKs income requirements; child care provided for children through age 12 (up to 21 for special needs)

name: California Child Care Subsidized Programs
description: State-funded child care and development subsidy programs providing vouchers for licensed child care centers, family child care homes, or license-exempt care for low-income families.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families needing child care assistance, including Alternative Payment Program participants
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility varies by program; generally at or below 85% of state median income

name: MyChildCarePlan (Child Care Resource & Referral Network)
description: Statewide search tool and support website to help California families find licensed child care. Connects to 58 local Resource & Referral agencies across every county.
eligibilitySummary: All California families seeking child care information and referrals
hours: Online 24/7; local R&R agencies have business hours

name: California WIC Program
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy foods, and referrals to health care for pregnant individuals, new parents, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum individuals, infants, and children under 5 who meet income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 185% of federal poverty level
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages at local clinics

name: LA County Department of Public Social Services (DPSS)
description: Administers CalFresh, Medi-Cal, CalWORKs, General Relief, IHSS, and other public assistance programs for Los Angeles County residents.
eligibilitySummary: Los Angeles County residents needing food, health, cash, or other social services
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: LA County General Relief
description: County-funded cash aid program for indigent adults without income or resources who are not eligible for federal or state assistance programs. Provides temporary financial support.
eligibilitySummary: Single adults or couples with no dependent children, no income, and no resources who are ineligible for other aid programs
incomeLimitNotes: Must have virtually no income or resources; GR is structured as a loan repayable from future SSI or home sale proceeds

name: California General Assistance
description: County-funded program providing temporary cash aid to indigent adults who are not eligible for federal or state assistance. Each county sets its own benefit amounts and rules.
eligibilitySummary: Adults with no income or resources who are ineligible for other federal or state programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by county; must have essentially no income or assets

name: In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS)
description: Provides in-home assistance to eligible aged, blind, and disabled individuals as an alternative to out-of-home care, enabling recipients to remain safely in their own homes.
eligibilitySummary: Aged (65+), blind, or disabled individuals who are on or eligible for Medi-Cal and need help with daily activities to remain at home
incomeLimitNotes: Must be eligible for Medi-Cal; specific income limits apply

name: California LifeLine Phone Program
description: Provides discounted home phone and cell phone service (up to $19/month discount) to qualifying low-income California households.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households or those enrolled in qualifying public assistance programs (Medi-Cal, CalFresh, CalWORKs, SSI, etc.)
incomeLimitNotes: Income-based or program-based eligibility; income threshold varies by household size

name: LA Metro Reduced Fare Program
description: Reduced transit fares for seniors 62+, Medicare recipients, and persons with disabilities on LA Metro bus and rail. Up to 80% discount off regular fares.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors age 62+, Medicare card holders, and persons with disabilities in Los Angeles County
incomeLimitNotes: No income limit; must meet age or disability criteria

name: TAP Reduced Fare Program (Los Angeles)
description: Los Angeles TAP card program providing reduced fare transit access across multiple LA County transit agencies for seniors, disabled riders, students, and low-income riders (LIFE program).
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 62+, persons with disabilities, students, and low-income riders in Los Angeles County
incomeLimitNotes: LIFE program for riders below federal poverty level; other categories based on age/disability

name: FTA Section 5310 - Elderly and Disabled Specialized Transit (California)
description: Federal program administered by the California Transportation Commission providing grants for specialized transit services for seniors and individuals with disabilities statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Transit agencies and nonprofits serving seniors and individuals with disabilities

name: CalVet (California Department of Veterans Affairs)
description: State department serving 1.6 million California veterans and their families with benefits assistance, veterans homes, home loans, education, and employment services.
eligibilitySummary: California veterans and their eligible family members
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; home loans require honorable discharge and 90+ days active duty

name: CalVet Veterans Services and Benefits
description: Comprehensive hub for California veteran benefits including claims assistance, education benefits, housing, employment support, and referrals to County Veterans Service Offices.
eligibilitySummary: California veterans, active service members, and their dependents/survivors
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by specific benefit program

name: LAHSA (Los Angeles Homeless Services Authority)
description: Lead agency coordinating housing and services for people experiencing homelessness across Los Angeles County, including emergency shelter, rapid rehousing, and permanent supportive housing.
address: 707 Wilshire Blvd, 10th Floor, Los Angeles, CA 90017
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness in Los Angeles County

name: Legal Services of Northern California (LSNC)
description: Federally funded legal services program providing free civil legal representation to low-income clients in 23 Northern California counties covering housing, benefits, family law, and more.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of 23 Northern California counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125-200% of federal poverty level
languages: English, Spanish, Hmong, and other languages

name: Legal Aid Society of San Diego
description: Nonprofit providing free legal assistance to low-income and vulnerable individuals and families in San Diego County in areas including housing, domestic violence, immigration, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income and vulnerable residents of San Diego County
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125-200% of federal poverty level

name: California Association of Food Banks
description: Statewide network of food banks working to end hunger in California through food distribution, policy advocacy, and connecting residents to CalFresh and nutrition programs.
eligibilitySummary: Any California resident in need of food assistance can be connected to a local food bank
